methods.description: Process of acquiring archival and newly collected surficial sediment samples from the UK EEZ for analysis.

steps.description: Acquire archival surficial sediment samples from the British Geological Survey (BGS) sample repository (Keyworth, United Kingdom), representing two distinct phases of sampling from the UK EEZ: UK Hydrographic Office (UKHO) programme (2005 onwards) and large scale BGS surveys (1970s and early 1980s).
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire archival sediment samples from BGS repository.

steps.description: Collect new Day grab samples in July 2019 from the continental shelf within the Scottish sector of the UK EEZ onboard the MRV Scotia, and from the Scottish fjords.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect new sediment samples using Day grab.

steps.description: Describe all collected samples (archival and new) according to a modified Folk classification scheme.
steps.searchable_summary: Classify sediment samples using Folk classification.

steps.description: Obtain associated data (additional grain size, geochemical data, calcium carbonate (CaCO3) concentration) for archival samples from the BGS repository descriptions.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain associated data (grain size, geochemistry, CaCO3) for archival samples.
outputs: Associated data (grain size, geochemistry, CaCO3) linked to archival samples

methods.description: Process for quantifying bulk sediment Organic Carbon (OC) and Inorganic Carbon (IC) content using the Elementar Soli TOC instrument and the temperature gradient method.

steps.description: Freeze dry the 619 surface samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Freeze dry sediment samples.

steps.description: Homogenize freeze dried samples to a fine powder.
steps.searchable_summary: Homogenize freeze-dried sediment samples.

steps.description: Weigh 30 mg of homogenized sample into a steel crucible.
steps.searchable_summary: Weigh sediment sample aliquot.

steps.description: Place crucible into the Elementar Soli TOC instrument.
steps.searchable_summary: Load sample into Soli TOC analyzer.
outputs: Soli TOC instrument loaded with sample

steps.description: Perform analysis using the temperature gradient method (DIN 19539), involving ramped heating of the sample at a rate of 70°C min−1 through sequential furnace temperatures of 400°C, 600°C and 900°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform temperature gradient analysis for carbon content using Soli TOC.
inputs: Soli TOC instrument loaded with sample
Temperature gradient method parameters (400°C, 600°C, 900°C, 70°C/min ramp rate)
outputs: CO2 evolution data at different temperature windows

steps.description: Measure the amount of CO2 produced from each of the temperature windows (0-400°C, 400-600°C, 600-900°C) by infrared spectrometry.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure CO2 evolution by infrared spectrometry.
outputs: Measured CO2 amounts for TOC, ROC, and TIC temperature windows

steps.description: Calculate Organic Carbon (OC) as the sum of Total Organic Carbon (TOC) (0-400°C CO2) and Residual Oxidizable Carbon (ROC) (400-600°C CO2).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Organic Carbon (OC) from TOC and ROC measurements.

steps.description: Calculate Inorganic Carbon (IC) as the Total Inorganic Carbon (TIC) value (600-900°C CO2).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Inorganic Carbon (IC) from TIC measurement.

steps.description: Perform quality control by analyzing triplicate measurements (n=20) and repeat analysis of standard reference material B2290 (n=50).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform quality control for carbon analysis using triplicates and standards.
outputs: Quality control data (standard deviations, deviations from reference values)

methods.description: Process of defining and delineating distinct spatial zones within the UK EEZ based on geographical features and environmental characteristics to reflect differences in sedimentological and biogeochemical processes.

steps.description: Define the overall study area encompassing the UK EEZ (743,959 km2) and associated UK Crown Dependencies (Isle of Man and Channel Islands, 10,057 km2).
steps.searchable_summary: Define study area boundaries.
inputs: Geographical coordinates/boundaries of UK EEZ and Crown Dependencies

steps.description: Identify key geographical features and environmental characteristics within the study area relevant to sediment properties and carbon content (e.g., fjords, coastlines, estuaries, continental shelf, shelf slope, deep sea, oceanographic currents like the Scottish Coastal Current).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify relevant geographical and environmental features.
inputs: Geological data of the study area

steps.description: Delineate four distinct zones within the study area: 1) fjords, 2) coastal and inshore, 3) continental shelf, and 4) shelf slope and deep sea.
steps.searchable_summary: Delineate spatial zones within the study area.

steps.description: Define the specific boundaries for each zone based on established criteria, such as geographical extent (e.g., Scottish sea lochs, Northern Irish sea loughs for fjords), distance from coastline (e.g., 5 km buffer for coastal/inshore), inclusion of large estuaries, influence of oceanographic currents (e.g., Scottish Coastal Current for coastal/shelf boundary), and bathymetric features (e.g., shelf break for shelf/deep sea boundary).
steps.searchable_summary: Define zone boundaries based on specific criteria.
outputs: Precise spatial boundaries for the four zones

methods.description: Process of compiling, screening, and processing existing surficial Organic Carbon (OC) and Inorganic Carbon (IC) data from various sources, including the use and validation of legacy calcium carbonate (CaCO3) data as an IC proxy.

steps.description: Compile surficial OC data from a variety of sources across the UK EEZ (Supplementary Table S2).
steps.searchable_summary: Compile existing surficial OC data from multiple sources.
inputs: Various data sources (databases, publications, Supplementary Table S2)

steps.description: Screen compiled OC data for quality control, ensuring only OC data collected from bulk samples are used; OC measured from specific size fractions are rejected.
steps.searchable_summary: Screen compiled OC data for quality control (bulk samples only).
inputs: Metadata indicating sample type (bulk vs. size fraction)

steps.description: Screen the filtered OC data to identify associated information, such as sediment descriptions (Folk class).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify associated sediment descriptions in OC data.
outputs: OC dataset with linked sediment descriptions

steps.description: Group the OC data points that have an associated sediment Folk class to determine the relationship between OC and sediment type for the UK EEZ (Supplementary Figure S3B).
steps.searchable_summary: Group OC data by sediment type for relationship analysis.
inputs: OC dataset with linked sediment descriptions
outputs: Grouped OC data by Folk class

steps.description: Group the remaining OC data points with no associated sediment class to be used as a groundtruthing and validation dataset (Supplementary Figure S4).
steps.searchable_summary: Group remaining OC data for validation.
inputs: Filtered OC dataset (excluding data used in step 4)

steps.description: Compile legacy BGS CaCO3 content data for seabed sediment samples collected in the 1970s and 1980s (Supplementary Figure S3C).
steps.searchable_summary: Compile legacy BGS CaCO3 data.

steps.description: Convert the legacy CaCO3 content to Inorganic Carbon (IC) content using simple stoichiometry, where 12% by mass of CaCO3 is C.
steps.searchable_summary: Convert CaCO3 content to Inorganic Carbon (IC).
outputs: Legacy IC dataset derived from CaCO3

steps.description: Compare the legacy CaCO3-derived IC values against the directly measured IC values (obtained from the Geochemical Analysis method) for the BGS archive sediment samples that were collected in this study, to test the reliability of the legacy data as a proxy for IC content.
steps.searchable_summary: Validate legacy CaCO3-derived IC data against direct IC measurements.
inputs: Directly measured IC dataset (from Method 2) for overlapping samples (BGS archive samples collected in this study)

steps.description: Sub-divide the compiled OC data with associated sediment descriptions (from step 4) based on spatial location (e.g., inshore vs. offshore environments) to overcome potential biasing of OC values within sediment classes.
steps.searchable_summary: Sub-divide OC data by sediment type and spatial location.
inputs: Grouped OC data by Folk class (from step 4)
outputs: Sub-divided OC data groups (e.g., Mud - Inshore, Mud - Offshore)
